build.detail: Build compiles successfully. 3 pre-existing asset size warnings (editor.js, screen bundle, css/screen).

issues.detail: Usage of __experimentalToggleGroupControl and __experimentalToggleGroupControlOption (lines 9-10). Pre-existing pattern used across multiple blocks.

lint.note: No lint errors specific to TestimonialsCarousel. The 2 warnings are for experimental WP component APIs, a pre-existing pattern across the theme.

registration.detail: block.json properly configured: name=takt/testimonials-carousel, apiVersion=3, 15 attributes defined with correct types and defaults, supports anchor. All required files exist: block.json, index.tsx, TestimonialsCarousel.tsx, TestimonialsCarousel.php, TestimonialsCarousel.svg, TestimonialsCarousel.js.

attributes.detail: All 15 attributes verified: anchor(string), eyebrow(string), heading(string), description(string), buttons(array), postsSource(string/enum), selectedPosts(array), postsLimit(number/default:9), hideIfEmpty(boolean), selectedPrograms(array), showPagination(boolean/default:true), showNavigation(boolean/default:true), loopCarousel(boolean), autoplayCarousel(boolean), autoplayDelay(number/default:3). Test page exercises multiple variations: automatic source with various limits, manual source with specific post IDs, program filtering, and hideIfEmpty.

edgeCases.detail: Test page covers: full automatic load (9 posts), limited load (6, 3), manual selection (3 specific posts), program-filtered query, hideIfEmpty with content present. Variation with no description/eyebrow/buttons renders correctly. Variation with all optional fields (eyebrow, description, buttons) renders correctly.

issues: FQA-001: Focus outline contrast in dark context (minor, theme-wide)
accessibility.detail: Strong accessibility implementation: section uses aria-label fallback when no heading, aria-labelledby when heading present; modal has role=dialog, aria-modal=true, aria-hidden toggling, dynamic aria-label with testimonial name; focus trap, escape key close, focus return to trigger element; close button has aria-label; nav buttons have aria-label and title; video buttons have descriptive aria-label with testimonial name; blockquote used for quotes; h3 for card names (proper h2>h3 hierarchy); decorative SVGs have aria-hidden=true.

markupDuplication.detail: No responsive duplication patterns found. No cloneNode or innerHTML-based content cloning.

tsxPhpSync.detail: TSX uses FilteredServerSideRender for the carousel area, so slides/modal are PHP-only. Wrapper section element, classes, container div classes, ThemeHeading props, and aria-label conditional all match between TSX and PHP. The modal and video functionality are frontend-only (PHP+JS) and correctly not duplicated in TSX.

innerBlocks.reason: Block does not use inner blocks

specCompliance.detail: Both changes from the planning spec are correctly implemented: (1) self-start removed from swiper-slide class (now 'swiper-slide h-auto!'), enabling equal-height cards via flexbox stretch; (2) close button restyled with carousel-nav-btn class, positioned at -top-5 -right-5 md:-top-6 md:-right-6, preserving .testimonial-video-close class for JS compatibility. Test page has 6 block instances covering all attribute variations.

issues.description: Video modal rendered 6 times instead of once. The static $testimonial_modal_rendered guard in TestimonialsCarousel.php is not preventing duplicate modal rendering across multiple block instances on the same page. The JS code uses document.querySelector('.testimonial-video-modal') which only selects the first one, but having 6 identical modal elements in the DOM is incorrect and wastes markup.
issues.fix: The static variable guard may not persist across include() calls from the render_callback closure. Consider using a global variable instead: replace 'static $testimonial_modal_rendered' with a check against a global or use wp_footer action to render the modal once. Alternative: use 'global $testimonial_modal_rendered; if (empty($testimonial_modal_rendered)) { $testimonial_modal_rendered = true;'
issues.evidence: Page audit found 6 .testimonial-video-modal elements in the DOM (one per block instance on the test page which has 6 blocks).

issues.description: Focus outline uses outline-black globally, which is invisible on the dark charcoal background of the carousel section (.dark context) and the dark modal backdrop. Carousel nav buttons and modal close button have poor focus visibility.
issues.fix: Add dark mode focus-visible override: .dark *:focus-visible { outline-color: white; } or use outline-current for context-adaptive focus rings. This is a theme-wide issue, not specific to this block.
issues.note: Theme-wide issue affecting all dark-context blocks.

issues.description: No prefers-reduced-motion handling for carousel autoplay, Swiper slide transitions, modal open/close animations, or carousel-nav-btn gradient sweep animation. Users who prefer reduced motion will still see all animations.
issues.fix: Check window.matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)') before enabling autoplay. Add @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) CSS rules to disable transitions and animations. This is a theme-wide pattern that should be addressed globally.
issues.note: Theme-wide issue affecting all animated blocks.

issues.description: Close button hover state changes text/border to charcoal via .carousel-nav-btn:hover rule, but button has explicit text-white border-white utilities. In Tailwind v4, the hover pseudo-class rule from the component layer would apply on hover, changing the button to charcoal text with gradient background. This provides adequate contrast (charcoal on bright gradient) but may look visually inconsistent with the white-on-dark rest state in the modal context.
issues.fix: Consider adding hover:text-charcoal hover:border-charcoal utilities explicitly on the close button for clarity, or alternatively override with hover:text-white hover:border-white if white-on-gradient is preferred in the modal context.

passedChecks: Block registers correctly with proper block.json configuration
All 15 attributes defined with correct types and defaults
Test page has 6 block instances covering automatic, manual, filtered, and hideIfEmpty variations
Strong accessibility: ARIA attributes on modal (role, aria-modal, aria-hidden, dynamic aria-label), focus trap, escape key, focus return, nav button labels
Semantic HTML: section wrapper, article cards, blockquote for quotes, proper heading hierarchy (h2>h3)
TSX/PHP sync verified (SSR pattern with FilteredServerSideRender)
Both spec changes implemented: equal-height cards (self-start removed) and restyled close button (carousel-nav-btn)
Video button has descriptive aria-label including testimonial name
Close button preserves .testimonial-video-close class for JS compatibility

variations: Automatic source, 9 posts, with pagination and navigation
Automatic source, 6 posts, with eyebrow, description, and button
Automatic source, 9 posts, defaults only (no explicit pagination/navigation attrs)
Automatic source, 6 posts, filtered by program taxonomy
